title: riscv-btor2 benchmark run manifest
description: BENCHMARKING.md §8.7 run manifest. The harness emits one of these per benchmark run; it is the join key between the corpus tag, the pinned solver/LLM inventory, and the §8.4 raw transcripts. Manifests are committed alongside results.

corpus_tag.description: git tag for the pre-registered corpus (BENCHMARKING.md §4.4). Identifies the (corpus, prompts, rubric, llm-inventory) bundle in the tagged commit.

corpus_commit.description: Full git sha of the tag.

schema_version.description: Pair SCHEMA.md version (e.g., 1.0.0). A bump invalidates prior manifests.

operator.description: Free-text identifier of the human who launched the run. Optional but recommended.

image.description: The §7 solver-version-pinning artifact. The digest is the load-bearing field; tags can be re-pointed.

tag.description: Convenience tag, e.g., the source commit short sha.

solvers.description: Redundant with the image digest but recorded explicitly so a human reading the manifest does not have to resolve the digest. If any value here disagrees with the image's actual contents, the digest is authoritative.

models.description: BENCHMARKING.md §7 requires ≥ 2 from unrelated families for §7-grade runs. The schema allows ≥ 1 to accommodate single-vendor exploratory manifests (e.g., v0.1.1 pipeline-soaks); such manifests must label themselves exploratory in the report. Slots match bench/riscv-btor2/llms.md.

snapshot.description: Dated snapshot id when the vendor exposes one; otherwise the model_id again.

params.description: Sampling/inference parameters. API-based slots SHOULD record temperature/top_p/max_tokens; claude-code-family slots leave params empty because the CLI controls those internally.

credential_fingerprint.description: env:VARNAME:LAST4 — proves the operator used a specific key without exfiltrating it.

rubric_llm.description: Optional: the §9.7 rubric LLM, distinct from the models under test. Shape matches a single models slot entry.

hardware.description: Where the harness ran (the LLM inference is remote and is not part of this).

docker.description: docker version output line, e.g., Docker Desktop 4.71.0.

determinism_check.description: Result of running bench/riscv-btor2/check_determinism.py (§9.9) before condition B/C started. sample_size==pass_count is required for a valid run.

runs.description: One entry per (task, condition, model_slot, seed) cell. Per BENCHMARKING.md §7 there are ≥5 entries with the same (task, condition, model_slot) tuple, differing only in seed.

question_id.description: Multi-question task: the [questions.qN] block this row covers. null for legacy single-question tasks.

transcript_path.description: Path inside the §8.4 artifact bundle, e.g., transcripts/0001-x0-write-dropped/B/slot_A/seed-42.json.

tool_calls.description: Total tool calls in the session (B/C only; 0 for A).

solver_seconds.description: Cumulative external-solver wall-clock seconds attributable to this run (BENCHMARKING.md §5). Currently always 0.0; see harness.py TODO.

graded.description: Payload from rubric/matcher.py Report.to_jsonable, with optional T4 rubric-LLM fields added by harness.grade.

coverage_gaps.description: Task ids the pair could not encode under condition B (BENCHMARKING.md §4.1). Reported as the coverage-gap rate in §5.

notes.description: Free-form prose. Anything material to interpreting the run that doesn't fit a structured field — incidents, manual interventions, vendor outages — goes here.
